summary: Vendor-neutral metered-usage ledger — record consumption events idempotently, aggregate per subject/meter/period, and report unreported usage to a biller (Stripe Meters) out-of-band. The ledger is the source of truth; the biller is an adapter.

exports: UsageMeter { record(input: RecordUsageInput): Promise<RecordedUsage>; total(query: UsageTotalQuery): Promise<UsageTotal>; summary(query: UsageSummaryQuery): Promise<UsageTotal[]>; reportDue(opts?: ReportDueOptions): Promise<UsageReport> }
class UsageError extends Error { code: UsageErrorCode }

invariants: Importing the part performs no I/O and never throws; record/total/summary validate input with typed errors, and record NEVER calls the biller inline — it only writes the local ledger, so a biller outage never blocks or fails metering

record is idempotent: the same idempotencyKey (per subject+meter) yields exactly one ledger row; the second call returns deduped:true with the original eventId. A non-finite or negative quantity is rejected before any SQL

total and summary aggregate correctly over the half-open [since, until) window (since-inclusive, until-exclusive), honor the subject/meter filters, and are deterministic; an empty range yields a zero total, not an error

Reporting is at-least-once toward the biller, deduplicated by the stable eventId (exactly-once within the biller's dedup window): reportDue reports each unreported event using its eventId as the biller idempotency key, then marks it reported; a biller failure leaves the event unreported for the next pass — never silently dropped — and a permanently-rejected event sinks in the drain order so it never starves fresh usage
Quantities round-trip exactly for integer units (no float drift); decimal handling is documented in seams.md
The biller secret never appears in error messages, in UsageError, or in any value the part returns

The part operates solely through the provided SqlExecutor seam — it imports no database driver — and every statement targets only its own billing_usage_events table; every input is parameterized, so SQL metacharacters are stored literally and never executed
notes: The last of the Wave 2 trio (after auth.apikey + webhooks.dispatch) that makes the AI-app / API-product skeleton (App Pack ai-api) assemblable. Specified by RFC 0005 (the capability was already in the docs/02 namespace). Vendor-neutral LEDGER core (zero-dep, owns billing_usage_events) + a Stripe Meters reporting adapter (per-adapter npm_dependencies stripe, RFC 0001). record() is enqueue-only and idempotent (ON CONFLICT (subject_id, meter, idempotency_key) DO NOTHING); total/summary aggregate over a half-open window; reportDue drains reported_at IS NULL rows to the biller exactly-once (the stable eventId is Stripe's meter-event identifier), report-before-mark, mirroring webhooks.dispatch.deliverDue — composes with jobs.queue or a plain cron, NO requires edge. quantity is NUMERIC (exact integers + decimals; node-postgres returns it as a string; the adapter reports the exact string not a rounded double). subjectId is opaque (customer/org id OR an auth.apikey id). Conformance: ledger idempotency/aggregation/injection vs real Postgres (gated PARTKIT_TEST_DATABASE_URL), the reportDue drain state-machine vs a fake recorder, and the stripe adapter vs the REAL Stripe test API (gated STRIPE_TEST_SECRET_KEY) — record meter events then poll listEventSummaries for the eventually-consistent aggregate; validation/redaction/own-table run DB-free. The suite lives with the implementing part until a second provider exists, then moves here (docs/02 §3-4).
